questions.question: Explain the difference between a page and a frame.
keyPoints: Page: Fixed-size block in logical/virtual address space
Frame: Fixed-size block in physical memory
They are the same size, determined by the hardware architecture

questions.question: Describe the address translation process in a paging system, including the role of the page table and the TLB. Calculate the Effective Access Time given a TLB hit ratio and memory access times.
keyPoints: CPU generates a logical address (page number + offset)
Hardware checks TLB for translation (TLB hit: fast, TLB miss: slow)
On TLB miss, page table in memory is accessed to find frame number
TLB is updated with the new translation
Physical address is formed (frame number + offset)
EAT calculation: (Hit Ratio * (TLB Time + MEM Time)) + (Miss Ratio * (TLB Time + 2*MEM Time))
